title: He Threw Away a $100,000 Lottery Ticket. Then He Went Dumpster Diving.
script: Trash or treasure? A man in Willard, Ohio bought a Bingo 25 Times 25 scratch-off ticket from the Circle K on Walton Street. He thought it was a dud — so he tossed it in the trash. But after family members moved the bag outside to a large community dumpster, he got suspicious. He climbed in. He dug around. He found the ticket at the bottom. It was worth $100,000. After required Ohio Lottery tax withholdings, he walked away with $73,250 — enough to buy a really good reason to start checking your scratch-offs before throwing them out. Officials confirmed the win was legitimate. The Ohio Lottery says this kind of mistake happens more often than people think. Did he deserve that $73,250, or should the trash have kept it?
